Sun, 01 Mar 2026 04:00:03 GMT  •  University of North Carolina Athletics
Lucas: Virginia Tech Rapid Reactions - University of North Carolina Athletics
🏀 Carolina defeated Virginia Tech 89-82, tying the program record for most home wins with 17 this season.
⛹️ Henri Veesaar scored 18 of his career-high tying 26 points after halftime, and Seth Trimble added 13 of his 20 points in the second half.
🎯 Carolina converted 23 of 30 free throw opportunities, including an 8-of-8 showing from Zayden High.
Sun, 01 Mar 2026 11:08:26 GMT  •  BBC
Israel and Iran exchange fresh attacks after Iran's Supreme Leader Khamenei killed
✈️ All flights from Dubai International and Dubai Al Maktoum were suspended until further notice due to airspace closures.
🛑 British Airways cancelled flights to Tel Aviv and Bahrain until Wednesday, with impacts expected on services to other Middle East cities.
🩹 Four staff members at Dubai International were injured in an incident overnight.
Sun, 01 Mar 2026 11:01:57 GMT  •  The Washington Post
Column | Asking Eric: Grandson has no motivation; daughter enables him
👨‍👩‍👦 The column addresses a reader's concern about their grandson's lack of motivation.
💬 The reader details their daughter's enabling behavior towards the grandson.
✍️ The column offers guidance on addressing family dynamics and fostering independence.
♦
Podcast Episodes
2026-03-01  •  Lex Fridman Podcast
#492 – Rick Beato: Greatest Guitarists of All Time, History & Future of Music
🎯 #492 – Rick Beato: Greatest Guitarists of All Time, History & Future of Music
2026-02-28  •  This Week in Startups
The Biggest Private Funding Round in History | E2256
💰 OpenAI reportedly raised $110 billion in private funding, with investments from Microsoft, Nvidia, and SoftBank.
📈 ChatGPT currently serves 900 million weekly active users and has 50 million paying subscribers.
🧠 Host Jason Calacanis claimed that AGI has already been achieved, though it is not yet fully implemented.
2026-02-28  •  AI Daily Brief
Who Controls AI?
💬 Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei publicly stated concerns about using AI models for autonomous weapons and mass domestic surveillance.
🚨 President Trump posted on Truth Social directing federal agencies to cease using Anthropic products.
🤝 OpenAI subsequently announced its own agreement with the Department of War, claiming similar safeguards were included.
2026-02-28  •  AI Daily Brief
Are 40% Staff Cuts the New AI Normal?
🍌 Google released a new model named Nano Banana 2.
📈 Claude AI experienced a surge in signups, indicating increased user adoption.
💻 Microsoft previewed its new Copilot Tasks feature.
2026-02-27  •  Morning Brew Daily
Netflix Walks Away From WBD Deal & Why is Everyone Leaving America?
🎬 Netflix walked away from a potential deal to acquire Warner Bros. Discovery after Paramount made a counter offer.
✂️ Jack Dorsey's company Block cut nearly half of its staff, attributing the reductions to AI's impact.
🏪 Papa John's is closing hundreds of stores, while Waymo is expanding its autonomous driving service into more cities.
♦
AI Security Papers
2026-02-26  •  Kunihiro Miyazaki, Takanobu Kawahara, Stephen Roberts et al.
Toward Expert Investment Teams:A Multi-Agent LLM System with Fine-Grained Trading Tasks
💡 The paper proposes a multi-agent LLM trading framework that explicitly decomposes investment analysis into fine-grained tasks.
📉 Mainstream multi-agent systems often use abstract instructions, leading to degraded inference performance and less transparent decision-making.
📈 The framework aims to improve upon existing systems mimicking analyst and manager roles by detailing real-world workflows.
2026-02-26  •  Sahal Shaji Mullappilly, Mohammed Irfan Kurpath, Omair Mohamed et al.
MediX-R1: Open Ended Medical Reinforcement Learning
🔬 MediX-R1 is introduced as an open-ended Reinforcement Learning framework for medical multimodal large language models (MLLMs).
💬 The framework enables clinically grounded, free-form answers in MLLMs, moving beyond conventional multiple-choice formats.
⚙️ MediX-R1 fine-tunes a baseline vision-language backbone with Group Based RL and a composite reward tailored for medical reasoning.
2026-02-26  •  Salim Fares
Utilizing LLMs for Industrial Process Automation
🖥️ Most current publications on LLMs in software engineering focus on widely-used general-purpose programming languages like Python.
🏭 The utility of LLMs for software in industrial process automation, which uses highly specialized proprietary languages, remains underexplored.
🎯 This research aims to assess the application of LLMs within proprietary industrial process automation domains.
2026-02-23  •  Nicole Cho, William Watson, Alec Koppel et al.
No One Size Fits All: QueryBandits for Hallucination Mitigation
🚫 QueryBandits is introduced as a model-agnostic contextual bandit framework designed to adaptively learn online methods for hallucination mitigation in LLMs.
🔍 Most existing hallucination mitigation research focuses on open-source models for post-hoc detection and parameter editing.
🏢 The framework addresses the lack of studies on hallucinations in closed-source LLMs predominantly used in institutional deployments.
2026-02-23  •  William Watson, Nicole Cho, Sumitra Ganesh et al.
What Makes a Good Query? Measuring the Impact of Human-Confusing Linguistic Features on LLM Performance
🧠 Large Language Model (LLM) hallucinations are usually treated as defects of the model or its decoding strategy.
📊 Drawing on classical linguistics, we argue that a query's form can also shape a listener's (and model's) response.
⚙️ We operationalize this insight by constructing a 22-dimension query feature vector covering clause complexity, lexical rarity, and anaphora, negation, answerability, and intention grounding, all known to affect human comprehension.
♦
AI Security News
Sat, 28 Feb 2026 17:21:00 GMT  •  The Hacker News
ClawJacked Flaw Lets Malicious Sites Hijack Local OpenClaw AI Agents via WebSocket
🛡️ OpenClaw fixed a high-severity security flaw, codenamed ClawJacked, in version 2026.2.25 released on February 26, 2026.
🔑 The vulnerability allowed malicious websites to brute-force a local OpenClaw gateway password due to a missing rate-limiting mechanism.
🚨 The flaw exploited local WebSocket connections to silently auto-approve new device registrations without requiring user confirmation.
Wed, 25 Feb 2026 23:00:43 GMT  •  TechTarget
LLM firewalls emerge as a new AI security layer
🔒 LLM firewalls are specialized tools designed to monitor, filter, and sanitize user input for AI-driven risks like prompt injection attacks and data leaks.
🧠 These firewalls differ from WAFs by analyzing the semantics, intent, and context of natural language in both incoming prompts and outgoing responses.
📐 An LLM firewall typically features a prompt firewall, a retrieval firewall for external data, and a response firewall for outbound generated text.
Tue, 24 Feb 2026 09:04:17 GMT  •  Qualys
DeepSeek Jailbreak Vulnerability Analysis | Qualys TotalAI
⚠️ DeepSeek-R1 LLaMA 8B variant failed over half of the jailbreak tests conducted by Qualys TotalAI.
🔎 Qualys TotalAI is an AI security platform that assesses AI models across 16 categories for security threats, safety concerns, and ethical risks.
📊 DeepSeek's distilled versions, built on Llama and Qwen, aim to offer competitive performance and resource efficiency.
Mon, 23 Feb 2026 08:00:00 GMT  •  csoonline.com
13 ways attackers use generative AI to exploit your systems
🎣 Generative AI enables cybercriminals to create highly convincing and personalized phishing emails, increasing success rates.
🐛 Attackers use gen AI to generate more sophisticated malware, such as malicious HTML documents for HTML smuggling in XWorm attacks.
🤖 AI is evolving from a mere 'helper' to an autonomous 'partner-in-crime' for attackers, capable of executing entire attack chains.

AI Security Papers
2026-02-26  •  Kunihiro Miyazaki, Takanobu Kawahara, Stephen Roberts et al.
Toward Expert Investment Teams:A Multi-Agent LLM System with Fine-Grained Trading Tasks
📈 The paper proposes a multi-agent LLM trading framework that explicitly decomposes investment analysis into fine-grained trading tasks.
🔍 Current mainstream multi-agent systems often rely on abstract instructions, which can lead to degraded inference performance and less transparent decision-making.
⚙️ The research aims to improve transparency and performance in autonomous financial trading systems by detailing real-world workflows.
2026-02-26  •  Sahal Shaji Mullappilly, Mohammed Irfan Kurpath, Omair Mohamed et al.
MediX-R1: Open Ended Medical Reinforcement Learning
🩺 MediX-R1 introduces an open-ended Reinforcement Learning framework for medical multimodal LLMs (MLLMs), enabling clinically grounded, free-form answers beyond multiple-choice formats.
🤖 The framework fine-tunes a baseline vision-language backbone using Group Based RL and a composite reward system tailored for medical reasoning.
📊 The composite reward incorporates an LLM-based accuracy reward for strict YES/NO semantic correctness and a medical embedding-based semantic reward to capture paraphrases.
2026-02-26  •  Salim Fares
Utilizing LLMs for Industrial Process Automation
🧠 A growing number of publications address the best practices to use Large Language Models (LLMs) for software engineering in recent years.
📊 However, most of this work focuses on widely-used general purpose programming languages like Python due to their widespread usage training data.
⚙️ The utility of LLMs for software within the industrial process automation domain, with highly-specialized languages that are typically only used in proprietary contexts, remains underexplored.
2026-02-26  •  Chen Bo Calvin Zhang, Christina Q. Knight, Nicholas Kruus et al.
LLM Novice Uplift on Dual-Use, In Silico Biology Tasks
🧪 A multi-model, multi-benchmark human uplift study compared novice users with LLM access versus internet-only access across eight biosecurity-relevant task sets.
🧠 Participants worked on computational biology tasks, demonstrating LLMs' potential to improve human performance in complex scientific domains.
🔬 The research aims to understand both scientific acceleration and dual-use risk in biology tasks when LLMs are utilized by novice users.
2026-02-26  •  Zeyuan Liu, Jeonghye Kim, Xufang Luo et al.
Exploratory Memory-Augmented LLM Agent via Hybrid On- and Off-Policy Optimization
🗺️ The paper identifies exploration as a key bottleneck for large language model agents trained with reinforcement learning, especially in environments requiring the discovery of novel states.
💡 It proposes Exploratory Memory-Augmented On- and Off-Policy Optimization (EMPO^2), a novel hybrid RL framework.
💪 EMPO^2 leverages memory for enhanced exploration and combines on- and off-policy updates to ensure LLMs perform well with memory while maintaining robustness without it.

AI Security News
Wed, 25 Feb 2026 21:08:41 GMT  •  TechTarget
LLM firewalls emerge as a new AI security layer
🛡️ LLM firewalls are emerging technologies designed to monitor, filter, and sanitize user input, manage model interactions, and track data flow to mitigate AI-driven risks.
⚠️ These firewalls protect against prompt injection attacks, data leaks, malicious code generation, privilege escalation, and model overuse.
⚙️ LLM firewalls differ from WAFs by analyzing the semantics, intent, and context of natural language in prompts and responses, typically with prompt, retrieval, and response components.
Tue, 24 Feb 2026 16:02:48 GMT  •  ReversingLabs
How AI agents upend supply chain security
🚨 AI agents introduce new software supply chain risks because their behavior depends on LLM interpretation of instructions at runtime, rather than deterministic compiled code.
🔑 Agents often possess administrative access to critical systems but lack essential security controls like cryptographic signatures, reputation systems, and audit trails found in traditional software.
marketplace 🛍️ New marketplaces like ClawHub distribute unvetted AI agents and skills, undermining established supply chain security practices based on SBOMs and secure development.

💡 Qualys TotalAI provides full visibility into AI workloads, proactively detects threats like prompt injection, and safeguards infrastructure for secure AI deployment.
Mon, 23 Feb 2026 08:00:00 GMT  •  csoonline.com
13 ways attackers use generative AI to exploit your systems

malware 💻 AI is being used to develop more sophisticated or less labor-intensive malware, exemplified by the creation of malicious HTML documents for attacks like XWorm.

Wed, 25 Feb 2026 23:49:19 +0000  •  TechCrunch
Anthropic acquires computer-use AI startup Vercept after Meta poached one of its founders
🛒 Anthropic acquired Vercept, an AI startup that developed Vy, a computer-use agent capable of operating a remote Apple MacBook in the cloud.
💰 Vercept had raised a total of $50 million, including a $16 million seed round from January of the prior year.
👋 Vercept co-founder Matt Deitke was previously poached by Meta with a $250 million salary to join its Superintelligence Lab.
Wed, 25 Feb 2026 19:10:04 +0000  •  TechCrunch
Wearable startup CUDIS launches a new health ring line with an AI-fueled ‘coach’
💍 CUDIS is launching a new series of health rings equipped with an AI \"agent coach\" that generates tailored exercise programs and recommendations.
🎁 The CUDIS ring incentivizes healthy behaviors with digital \"health points\" redeemable for discounts on health supplements and products.
📈 CUDIS CEO Edison Chen reported selling over 30,000 units of previous models since 2024 and expanding to 250,000 users across 103 countries.
2026-02-25T14:56:55-05:00  •  The Verge
Google and Samsung just launched the AI features Apple couldn’t with Siri
📱 Google announced Gemini will perform multistep tasks, like ordering food or hailing a car, launching first on Pixel 10, Pixel 10 Pro, and Samsung Galaxy S26 phones.
🍕 Google demonstrated Gemini taking a pizza order from a family group chat and placing it through Grubhub, requiring user review before submission.
⌚ Apple's similar Siri features, announced at WWDC 2024 to understand screen context and take cross-app actions, were delayed in March 2025 and remain unreleased.
Thu, 26 Feb 2026 11:31:16 GMT  •  The Washington Post
Iran, U.S. resume nuclear negotiations as Trump’s war clock ticks down
🇮🇷 Iran and the U.S. have resumed nuclear negotiations.
⏰ The resumption of talks is occurring as \"Trump's war clock\" ticks down.
🕊️ The negotiations imply an ongoing diplomatic effort between the two nations on nuclear issues.
Thu, 26 Feb 2026 06:00:00 -0500  •  NPR
Pentagon shifts toward maintaining ties to Scouting
🏛️ Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth is easing efforts to sever the Pentagon's century-long relationship with Scouting America.
🚫 Hegseth had previously proposed barring Scout troops from U.S. bases and ending military assistance for the Scout Jamboree.
🤝 Pentagon demands for continuing the partnership include requiring new scouts to register under their sex assigned at birth.

Podcast Episodes
2026-02-26  •  This Week in Startups
Behind the Scenes with an early OpenClaw contributor! | E2252
📊 Tyler Yust, the third-ever contributor to OpenClaw, shared his insights into the AI agent platform's foundation.

AI Security Papers
2026-02-25  •  Hanna Yukhymenko, Anton Alexandrov, Martin Vechev
Recovered in Translation: Efficient Pipeline for Automated Translation of Benchmarks and Datasets
📄 The paper introduces a fully automated framework designed for scalable, high-quality translation of datasets and benchmarks.
⚠️ The framework addresses the issue of inconsistent quality, semantic drift, and context loss in existing translated multilingual LLM evaluation benchmarks.
⚙️ The research demonstrates that adapting test-time compute scaling strategies improves the quality of translated benchmarks.
2026-02-25  •  Minqiu Sun, Xin Huang, Luanzheng Guo et al.
LLMTailor: A Layer-wise Tailoring Tool for Efficient Checkpointing of Large Language Models
💾 The paper proposes LLMTailor, a layer-wise tailoring tool for efficient checkpointing that optimizes storage based on non-uniform updates across LLM layers during training.
📦 Existing checkpointing methods incur substantial storage overhead by periodically storing entire model and optimizer states.
🔄 The research reveals that some LLM layers undergo more significant changes, while others remain relatively stable, across training steps.
2026-02-25  •  Leon Pielage, Ole Hätscher, Mitja Back et al.
Dynamic Personality Adaptation in Large Language Models via State Machines
🤖 The paper proposes a model-agnostic framework for dynamic personality simulation in Large Language Models (LLMs) using state machines to represent latent personality states.
💡 Transition probabilities between personality states are dynamically adapted to the conversational context.
📈 The architecture includes a modular pipeline for continuous personality scoring within the LLM framework.
2026-02-25  •  Yining Li, Peizhong Ju, Ness Shroff
Provable Last-Iterate Convergence for Multi-Objective Safe LLM Alignment via Optimistic Primal-Dual
safety
🧠 Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback (RLHF) for aligning Large Language Models (LLMs) can be formulated as a primal-dual optimization problem with expected reward constraints.
🚫 Standard primal-dual methods only guarantee convergence with a distributional policy when the saddle-point problem is in a convex-concave form.
📉 The paper addresses the instability or divergence of standard primal-dual methods in the last iterate under policy parameterization.
2026-02-23  •  Lingwei Gu, Nour Jedidi, Jimmy Lin
NanoKnow: How to Know What Your Language Model Knows
📚 The paper introduces NanoKnow, a benchmark dataset designed to understand how knowledge is encoded by Large Language Models (LLMs).
❓ NanoKnow partitions questions to provide transparency into where a model's parametric knowledge originates.
🔍 The benchmark utilizes nanochat, a family of small LLMs with fully open pre-training data, to shed light on knowledge sources.
♦
AI Security News
Wed, 25 Feb 2026 03:59:52 GMT  •  CybersecurityNews
GitHub Copilot Exploited to Perform Full Repository Takeover via Passive Prompt Injection
🚨 Researchers at Orca Research Pod uncovered RoguePilot, a critical vulnerability allowing full GitHub repository takeover via passive prompt injection in GitHub Codespaces.
💬 RoguePilot exploits HTML comment tags () in GitHub Issues to embed malicious instructions, invisible to humans but legible to Copilot.
🔓 The attack exfiltrates the GITHUB_TOKEN by making Copilot execute `gh pr checkout` to pull a symlinked file, then sending it to an attacker-controlled server via a JSON schema request.
Tue, 24 Feb 2026 09:04:17 GMT  •  Qualys
DeepSeek Jailbreak Vulnerability Analysis | Qualys TotalAI
📉 The DeepSeek-R1 LLaMA 8B variant failed over half of the jailbreak tests conducted by Qualys TotalAI, indicating significant security concerns.
🔬 Qualys TotalAI's Knowledge Base (KB) Analysis prompts target LLMs with questions across 16 categories to evaluate responses for vulnerabilities, ethical concerns, and legal risks.
💡 DeepSeek-R1 is a groundbreaking Large Language Model released by Chinese startup DeepSeek, with multiple distilled versions for various use cases.
Mon, 23 Feb 2026 11:20:25 GMT  •  Security Boulevard
Protecting AI Security: 2025 Hot Security Incident
💻 In May 2025, Invariant disclosed a critical GitHub Machine Collaboration Protocol (MCP) vulnerability, allowing attackers to embed commands in public Issues to hijack AI Agents and exfiltrate private repository data.
🔑 The GitHub MCP vulnerability bypassed permission controls, enabling unauthorized theft of private source code and cryptographic keys due to a lack of trust boundary definitions.
📧 In August 2025, Perplexity’s AI browser Comet was vulnerable to indirect prompt injection via Reddit spoiler tags, enabling AI to log into user email and transmit credentials in 150 seconds.
Mon, 23 Feb 2026 08:00:00 GMT  •  csoonline.com
13 ways attackers use generative AI to exploit your systems
🎣 Cybercriminals are using generative AI to create more sophisticated and personalized phishing emails by leveraging targeted information from social media.
🦠 AI is being deployed to develop more effective malware, such as malicious HTML documents for HTML smuggling attacks like the XWorm attack.
🤖 Agentic AI is evolving from a simple 'helper' to an autonomous 'partner-in-crime,' capable of executing entire attack chains for cybercriminals.

When a horse whinnies, there's more than meets the ear

🔬 This dual-frequency vocalization is common in birds but uncommon among mammals when consistently present in one type of sound.
anatom anatomical features allow horses to produce the low frequency via vibrating vocal folds and the high frequency via constricting cartilage above the larynx.

Podcast Episodes
2026-02-24  •  This Week in Startups
Kill Your Startup’s Knowledge Chaos with OpenClaw (with Oliver Henry and Jeff Weisbein) | E2254

2026-02-24  •  AI Daily Brief
The Rise of the Anti-AI Movement
👥 The "anti-AI movement" is composed of distinct groups, shaped by economic anxiety and disillusionment with social media.
💡 Most critics are not anti-technology ideologues but are addressing real, solvable concerns related to AI.

Fictional Story Tanks Stock Market & The iPod Making a Comeback?

AI Security Papers
2026-02-24  •  Renjie Pi, Grace Lam, Mohammad Shoeybi et al.
On Data Engineering for Scaling LLM Terminal Capabilities
🛠️ The paper introduces Terminal-Task-Gen, a lightweight synthetic task generation pipeline that supports seed-based and skill-based task construction.
🔍 The research systematically studies data engineering practices for LLM terminal agents, addressing undisclosed training data strategies.
📊 The paper provides a comprehensive analysis of data and training strategies for LLM terminal agents.
2026-02-24  •  Anas Barakat, Souradip Chakraborty, Khushbu Pahwa et al.
Why Pass@k Optimization Can Degrade Pass@1: Prompt Interference in LLM Post-training
✅ Pass@k is a metric for verifiable LLM tasks, such as code generation, that indicates success if any of k sampled solutions pass a verifier.
📉 Prior work consistently shows that methods optimizing pass@k often degrade pass@1 performance.
🧠 This trade-off is primarily attributed to prompt interference during the post-training phase of LLMs.
2026-02-24  •  Taiqiang Wu, Zenan Zu, Bo Zhou et al.
The Art of Efficient Reasoning: Data, Reward, and Optimization
🧠 Efficient reasoning aims to promote short, accurate thinking trajectories in LLMs, typically using reward shaping with Reinforcement Learning.
💡 The paper conducts a systematic investigation into the mechanics of efficient reasoning for LLMs.
📏 The authors advocate for using more fine-grained metrics, including length, for a comprehensive evaluation of efficient reasoning.
2026-02-22  •  Shiyi Cao, Ziming Mao, Joseph E. Gonzalez et al.
K-Search: LLM Kernel Generation via Co-Evolving Intrinsic World Model
🧠 Optimizing GPU kernels is critical for efficient modern machine learning systems yet remains challenging due to the complex interplay of design factors and rapid hardware evolution.
📊 Existing automated approaches typically treat Large Language Models (LLMs) merely as stochastic code generators within heuristic-guided evolutionary loops.
⚙️ These methods often struggle with complex kernels requiring coordinated, multi-step structural transformations, as they lack explicit planning capabilities and fr.
2026-02-18  •  Chia-chi Hsieh, Zan Zong, Xinyang Chen et al.
FlowPrefill: Decoupling Preemption from Prefill Scheduling Granularity to Mitigate Head-of-Line Blocking in LLM Serving
🚦 Head-of-Line (HoL) blocking in LLM serving systems causes widespread time-to-first-token (TTFT) SLO violations for concurrent requests.
🔄 While chunked prefill allows interruptibility, it introduces an inherent trade-off in responsiveness.
⚡ FlowPrefill mitigates HoL blocking by decoupling preemption from prefill scheduling granularity.
♦
AI Security News
Wed, 25 Feb 2026 03:59:52 GMT  •  CybersecurityNews
GitHub Copilot Exploited to Perform Full Repository Takeover via Passive Prompt Injection
🚨 RoguePilot, a critical AI vulnerability, enabled attackers to hijack GitHub repositories by embedding malicious instructions in GitHub Issues.
⚙️ The flaw exploited GitHub Copilot's automatic processing of issue descriptions as an initial prompt when a Codespace was launched.
🔓 The attack exfiltrated the GITHUB_TOKEN by making Copilot execute a pull request with a symlink to user secrets and then fetch a remote JSON schema with the token as a URL parameter.
Tue, 24 Feb 2026 19:40:32 GMT  •  ReversingLabs
How AI agents upend software supply chain security
⚠️ Autonomous AI agents introduce a new category of software supply chain risk due to their unpredictable behavior at runtime.
🕵️ Unlike traditional software with deterministic behavior, AI agents' actions are determined by how a large language model interprets instructions.
🛡️ New marketplaces like ClawHub distribute unvetted AI agents and skills lacking traditional security features like signatures, reputation systems, and audit trails.
Mon, 23 Feb 2026 11:20:25 GMT  •  Security Boulevard
Protecting AI Security: 2025 Hot Security Incident
🔒 A GitHub MCP vulnerability in May 2025 allowed attackers to embed malicious commands in public Issues, hijacking locally running AI Agents to exfiltrate private repository data.
💻 Perplexity’s AI browser Comet was exposed to an indirect prompt injection in August 2025, allowing AI to log into user emails and transmit credentials in 150 seconds.
💣 In November 2025, Oligo Security revealed attackers exploited the Ray framework (CVE-2023-48022) to generate AI-assisted malware for 230,000+ computing clusters.
Mon, 23 Feb 2026 09:58:42 GMT  •  Cyber Press
Study Finds LLM-Generated Passwords Highly Predictable and Repetitive
🚫 AI firm Irregular's analysis reveals that LLM-generated passwords from Claude, GPT, and Gemini are fundamentally weak due to predictable patterns and low entropy.
🔑 Claude Opus 4.6 repeated the password "G7$kL9#mQ2&xP4!w" 18 out of 50 times (36% chance) when prompted to generate a password.
📈 A 16-character Claude password yields only 27 bits of Shannon entropy, compared to an expected 98 bits for true randomness.

Top News
Mon, 23 Feb 2026 11:27:45 GMT  •  BBC News
Violence erupts in Mexico after killing of country's most wanted drug lord El Mencho
Mencho, Mexico's most wanted man, was seriously injured in a firefight between his bodyguards and the military commandos deployed to capture him. He died while the military was transporting him from the town of Tapalpa, in Jalisco state, to the capital, Mexico City. At least six of El Mencho's security guards were also killed in the operation, while three members of the Mexican military were injured, the defence ministry said.
Mon, 23 Feb 2026 11:02:00 GMT  •  CNBC
Stock futures fall amid uncertainty about Trump new tariffs: Live updates
l. U. S.
Mon, 23 Feb 2026 10:45:09 GMT  •  BBC News
'Confront failure and fix it': Families speak ahead of Nottingham attacks inquiry
who had been diagnosed with paranoid schizophrenia. Barnaby's mother Emma Webber has called for "accountability right down to an individual level" at the statutory public inquiry in London, which is due to run until May. Calocane is serving a hospital order after pleading guilty to three counts of manslaughter on the grounds of diminished responsibility, and three counts of attempted murder.
Mon, 23 Feb 2026 10:41:49 GMT  •  BBC News
Netflix boss defends bid for Warner Bros as Paramount deadline looms
akeover offer from Netflix for some of its assets, but Paramount has made a rival offer. Last week, Warner Bros gave Paramount until the end of Monday to submit a "best and final" offer ahead of the shareholder vote on the Netflix deal next month. Netflix has offered $27.
Mon, 23 Feb 2026 05:30:00 -0500  •  NPR
U.S. has a quarter fewer immigration judges than it did a year ago. Here's why
for months: she had been fired. As her phone began blowing up, she learned that six of the judges she supervised had been terminated as well. "As I tried to reassure them, I was trying to explain, 'But I've been terminated too,'" said Khan, who had been an assistant chief immigration judge at the court.
♦
Podcast Episodes
2026-02-22  •  AI Daily Brief
Why AI Could Be Better for Plumbers than Programmers
AI is reshaping the economy—but not always in the way most leaders expect. This episode explores why AI could matter more for plumbers than programmers, shifting leverage to trade entrepreneurs by removing operational friction rather than replacing skilled labor. From Gen Z’s growing pivot toward the trades to the rise of agentic tools that unlock scale without headcount, the real opportunity isn’
2026-02-21  •  This Week in Startups
We Asked 3 Experts How to Get More Value out of OpenClaw | E2253
Jordy Coltman claims new hardware is the optimal environment for AI agents, advocating for agents to be freed from screens and become proactive. Jesse Leimgruber demonstrated his OpenHome AI smart speakers, while Tremaine Grant showcased his "virtual office" interface and the "Heartbeat Protocol."
2026-02-20  •  AI Daily Brief
Does Gemini 3.1 Pro Matter?
Gemini 3.1 Pro arrives with big benchmark gains and a sharp jump in reasoning, coding, and efficiency—but in a world where the frontier rotates weekly, raw performance isn’t the story. This episode looks at what actually matters: cost per task, multimodal dominance, and where Gemini fits in a model portfolio that now demands specialization over supremacy. In the headlines: India’s AI Impact Summit
2026-02-20  •  Morning Brew Daily
Tariffs Ineffective Against US Trade Deficit? & Family Feud Over Reese’s Recipe
Neal and Toby discussed that Trump's aggressive tariffs have not reduced the swelling trade deficit, while the grandson of the Reese's Peanut Butter Cup inventor publicly criticized Hershey's for using cheaper ingredients. Etsy sold its secondhand marketplace to eBay, leading investors to cheer and making it the Stock of the Week, and Amazon snapped a 9-day losing streak, recovering from a $450 billion market value loss.
2026-02-19  •  This Week in Startups
When Will Openclaw go Mainstream? | E2252
Matthew believes Openclaw is not yet ready for consumer adoption, noting only 10% of people are technical enough to install it. Ryan uses Openclaw in his personal life and predicts a new Openclaw fork is emerging, while Anthropic has patched the ability to use Openclaw through its Pro plan. Jason described his "Clawpod" workflow, and Matt used Openclaw to identify his stomach issues.
♦
AI Security Papers
2026-02-20  •  Harshul Raj Surana, Arijit Maji, Aryan Vats et al.
VIRAASAT: Traversing Novel Paths for Indian Cultural Reasoning
The VIRAASAT paper proposes a new benchmark to evaluate Large Language Models (LLMs) on Indian cultural reasoning. It reveals that LLMs' performance deteriorates significantly in tasks requiring rich socio-cultural knowledge and diverse local contexts specific to Indian Culture.
2026-02-20  •  Jiamin Yao, Eren Gultepe
SPQ: An Ensemble Technique for Large Language Model Compression
SPQ (SVD-Pruning-Quantization) introduces an ensemble technique for Large Language Model (LLM) compression, combining variance-retained singular value decomposition (SVD), activation-based pruning, and post-training linear 8-bit quantization. This method reduces attention projections into compact low-rank factors and uniformly compresses all linear layers, enhancing efficiency.
2026-02-20  •  Cathy Mengying Fang, Sheer Karny, Chayapatr Archiwaranguprok et al.
AI-Wrapped: Participatory, Privacy-Preserving Measurement of Longitudinal LLM Use In-the-Wild
privacy safety
AI-Wrapped presents a prototype workflow for participatory, privacy-preserving measurement of longitudinal LLM use in real-world settings. It enables the collection of naturalistic LLM usage data while providing participants with immediate "wrapped"-style reports on their usage statistics and safety-relevant behavioral patterns.
2026-02-20  •  Alexandra Neagu, Marcus Messer, Peter Johnson et al.
"How Do I ...?": Procedural Questions Predominate Student-LLM Chatbot Conversations
The paper analyzes student questions in Large Language Model (LLM) chatbot conversations across two distinct learning contexts. It finds that "procedural questions" (e.g., "How do I...?") predominantly shape user prompts and drive the pedagogical effectiveness of chatbot responses when students encounter impasses.
2026-02-16  •  Dongrui Liu, Yi Yu, Jie Zhang et al.
Frontier AI Risk Management Framework in Practice: A Risk Analysis Technical Report v1.5
agentic AI
Frontier AI Risk Management Framework in Practice presents a comprehensive assessment of unprecedented risks posed by rapidly advancing AI models, including LLMs and agentic AI. This technical report offers an updated and granular assessment across five critical dimensions, such as cyber offense and persuasion/manipulation.
♦
AI Security News
Mon, 23 Feb 2026 11:15:00 GMT  •  Security Boulevard
Protecting AI Security: 2025 Hot Security Incident
In May 2025, Invariant discovered a critical GitHub Machine Collaboration Protocol (MCP) vulnerability, allowing attackers to embed malicious commands in public Issues which AI Agents would execute, exfiltrating sensitive data like private source code and cryptographic keys.
Mon, 23 Feb 2026 09:55:07 GMT  •  Cyber Press
Study Finds LLM-Generated Passwords Highly Predictable and Repetitive
AI firm Irregular's analysis reveals passwords generated by LLMs like Claude, GPT, and Gemini are fundamentally weak and predictable, with Claude Opus 4.6 repeating one string 18 times out of 50 attempts, a 36% chance. These passwords, which have low Shannon entropy (e.g., 27 bits for a 16-character Claude password), can be cracked in hours by prioritizing LLM patterns, with dozens of exposed examples found on GitHub.
Mon, 23 Feb 2026 06:48:52 GMT  •  더에이아이
Who Locks the Door After Handing the Keys to an AI Agent?
A cyber-espionage campaign disclosed in September 2025 by Anthropic revealed an attacker used Claude Code to target over 30 entities, with 80% of tactical tasks (like reconnaissance and data exfiltration) executed autonomously by the AI after a single click. The AI processed thousands of requests per second, highlighting that only 14.4% of organizations deploy agents with formal security approval, creating a new attack surface.
Thu, 19 Feb 2026 21:33:18 GMT  •  The New Stack
AI agents are accelerating vulnerability discovery. Here’s how AppSec teams must adapt.
XBOW, an autonomous AI penetration tester, submitted over 1,060 vulnerabilities in 90 days, leading to 130 critical vulnerabilities resolved by companies on HackerOne’s US leaderboard. JPMorgan Chase’s Auspex system uses generative AI with “tradecraft prompting” to perform threat modeling, collapsing the timeline from weeks to minutes while improving analysis quality.

Sun, 22 Feb 2026 11:19:10 GMT  •  BBC News
Your guide to the final day
The 25th Winter Olympics at Milan-Cortina has featured almost 3,000 athletes from 90 countries competing for 116 medals. Team GB sent 53 athletes and won a record three golds plus a silver, with the final day on Sunday, February 22, featuring five medal events including the men's ice hockey final between Canada and USA.
Sun, 22 Feb 2026 06:08:48 -0500  •  NPR
Danish military evacuates US submariner who needed urgent medical care off Greenland
The Danish Joint Arctic Command used a Seahawk helicopter to evacuate a U.S. submariner for urgent medical treatment 7 nautical miles off Nuuk, Greenland, on Saturday. This occurred shortly before former President Donald Trump announced plans on Truth Social to deploy a hospital ship to Greenland, claiming many people there are sick, which Denmark's Prime Minister Mette Frederiksen publicly refuted.
Sun, 22 Feb 2026 10:53:31 GMT  •  BBC News
'Effective' SEND support won't be taken away, minister says
Education Secretary Bridget Phillipson stated the government will spend more money, not less, on Special Educational Needs and Disabilities (SEND) support in England. A **White Paper on Monday** will detail plans for a **decade-long transition**, including reassessing children with Education, Health and Care Plans (EHCPs) after primary school starting 2029, and introducing new legally-underpinned Individual Support Plans (ISPs) for all SEND children.
Sun, 22 Feb 2026 05:00:00 -0500  •  NPR
Only a fraction of House seats are competitive. Redistricting is driving that lower
Only 18 out of 435 House races are rated as "toss-ups" by the Cook Political Report, meaning less than 5% of Americans will truly decide control of the House this fall. **Mid-decade redistricting efforts** initiated by former President Trump are largely blamed, with **32 states** currently having no competitive congressional races.
Sun, 22 Feb 2026 09:42:00 GMT  •  The Wall Street Journal
Trump Says He Is Sending Hospital Ship to Greenland
Former President Donald Trump announced plans to deploy a hospital ship to Greenland. He cited concerns that many people there are sick and not receiving care.
♦
Podcast Episodes
2026-02-21  •  This Week in Startups
We Asked 3 Experts How to Get More Value out of OpenClaw | E2253
**Jordy Coltman** (creator of WeeklyClaw) identified new hardware as the best home for AI agents, criticizing new users for common time-wasting mistakes. **Tremaine Grant** (Founder/CEO of PulsePLUS) demoed his "virtual office" interface and "Heartbeat Protocol," suggesting AI agents can **"chat" together** to accomplish tasks. **Jesse Leimgruber** (Creator of OpenHome.com) showcased his OpenHome AI smart speakers, aiming to make agents proactive and free from screens. Jason Calacanis predicted "OpenClaw is a box" and wants his agent to read Slack and emails to him.
2026-02-20  •  AI Daily Brief
Does Gemini 3.1 Pro Matter?
This podcast episode was dedicated to discussing the significance and impact of Google's Gemini 3.1 Pro model in the current AI landscape. The conversation aimed to ascertain whether the model presents a **substantial shift or improvement** in artificial intelligence capabilities. Listeners were intended to gain clarity on the model's relevance and its **potential future role in the industry**.
2026-02-20  •  Morning Brew Daily
Tariffs Ineffective Against US Trade Deficit? & Family Feud Over Reese’s Recipe
Neal and Toby discussed the swelling U.S. trade deficit despite former President Trump's aggressive tariffs, suggesting they have been **ineffective**. The grandson of the Reese's Peanut Butter Cup inventor publicly criticized Hershey's for **skimping on ingredients** to use cheaper alternatives. **Etsy sold its secondhand marketplace to eBay**, a move investors cheered, making Etsy the "Stock of the Week," while Amazon snapped a 9-day losing streak that cost it **$450 billion in market value**.
2026-02-19  •  This Week in Startups
When Will Openclaw go Mainstream? | E2252
**Matthew** argued that **OpenClaw is not yet ready for consumers**, citing only **10% of people are technical enough** to install it. **Ryan** suggested OpenClaw could offer consumers **unprecedented opportunities**, while **Jason Calacanis** envisioned a "Clawpod" to manage notifications from Slack and emails. It was revealed that **Anthropic patched the ability to use OpenClaw through its pro plan**, and **Peter Steinberger**, OpenClaw's founder, is moving to **OpenAI**, raising questions about the project's future.
2026-02-19  •  AI Daily Brief
How People Actually Use AI Agents
This podcast episode focused on exploring the practical applications and **real-world usage patterns of AI agents**. The discussion aimed to illuminate **how individuals and businesses currently deploy and interact with these autonomous systems**. It likely covered various scenarios where AI agents deliver **tangible benefits and operational efficiencies**, and explored their everyday utility.
♦
AI Security Papers
2026-02-18  •  Johannes Bertram, Jonas Geiping
NESSiE: The Necessary Safety Benchmark -- Identifying Errors that should not Exist
safety
NESSiE introduces a **NEceSsary SafEty benchmark** for LLMs using minimal test cases in information and access security. It reveals **safety-relevant failures** that should not exist, finding that even **state-of-the-art models fail** these low-complexity tasks.
2026-02-18  •  Sasha Behrouzi, Lichao Wu, Mohamadreza Rostami et al.
NeST: Neuron Selective Tuning for LLM Safety
safety
NeST (Neuron Selective Tuning) is a new safety alignment method for LLMs that focuses on efficient updates. It avoids the high computational and storage overhead of full fine-tuning, offering a **more efficient and consistent alternative** to parameter-efficient methods like LoRA.
2026-02-17  •  Zarif Ikram, Arad Firouzkouhi, Stephen Tu et al.
CrispEdit: Low-Curvature Projections for Scalable Non-Destructive LLM Editing
CrispEdit presents a **scalable and principled second-order editing algorithm** for LLMs that treats capability preservation as an explicit constraint. It successfully changes targeted behavior while **preventing corruption of general capabilities** and unifying existing editing approaches.
2026-02-16  •  Dongrui Liu, Yi Yu, Jie Zhang et al.
Frontier AI Risk Management Framework in Practice: A Risk Analysis Technical Report v1.5
agentic AI
The Frontier AI Risk Management Framework in Practice v1.5 provides a comprehensive assessment of risks posed by rapidly advancing AI models, including LLMs and agentic AI. This updated report offers a granular assessment across **five critical dimensions**, such as cyber offense and persuasion/manipulation.
2026-02-16  •  Yixin Zhang, Ziyi Wang, Yiming Rong et al.
World Models for Policy Refinement in StarCraft II
This paper proposes a method that integrates a **learnable, action-conditioned transition model** into the decision loop for LLM-based StarCraft II agents. This approach enhances decision-making in the complex environment, bridging a gap in existing LLM-based SC2 agents by going beyond just policy improvement with a **world model**.
♦
AI Security News
Fri, 20 Feb 2026 17:30:32 GMT  •  Security Boulevard
Using threat modeling and prompt injection to audit Comet
Perplexity hired security researchers to audit their **Comet browser's AI features** before launch, using adversarial testing with their **TRAIL threat model**. The audit demonstrated four prompt injection techniques could exploit the AI assistant to **extract users' private Gmail information**, highlighting how AI agents mishandle untrusted external input.
Thu, 19 Feb 2026 21:33:18 GMT  •  The New Stack
AI agents are accelerating vulnerability discovery. Here’s how AppSec teams must adapt.
**XBOW, an autonomous AI penetration tester**, submitted over 1,060 vulnerabilities in 90 days, leading to **130 critical vulnerabilities resolved** via bug bounty programs. In 2025, autonomous agents submitted over 560 valid reports, accelerating vulnerability discovery at machine scale, while **JPMorgan Chase's Auspex system** reduces threat modeling from weeks to **minutes**.
Thu, 19 Feb 2026 14:00:15 GMT  •  Institutional Investor
OpenClaw: The AI Agent Institutional Investors Need to Understand — But Shouldn't Touch
**OpenClaw**, an open-source AI agent formerly **Clawdbot and Moltbot**, has 300,000 to 400,000 users since its **November 2025 release**. Despite its capability to automate complex tasks like email management and code deployment, OpenClaw is not enterprise-ready due to security vulnerabilities, a lack of governance, and architecture incompatible with fiduciary responsibility.
Tue, 17 Feb 2026 10:04:06 GMT  •  csoonline.com
Why 2025’s agentic AI boom is a CISO’s worst nightmare
By late **2025**, standard Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) systems were failing at an 80% rate, prompting a shift to autonomous AI agents for enterprise solutions. This shift introduces risks like **malicious instruction execution**, data leaks, and endless loops, with **72% to 80% of enterprise RAG implementations** underperforming or failing within their first year.

Top News
Sat, 21 Feb 2026 21:05:18 GMT  •  BBC News
Nasa astronauts' moon mission delayed due to rocket issue
NASA's Artemis II mission, aiming to send four astronauts on a 10-day trip around the Moon for the first time in 50 years, is delayed from its early March launch window. NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman confirmed the delay Saturday after engineers found an interruption in helium flow during a 50-hour fueling test that used 730,000 gallons of propellant. This technical issue, involving helium for fuel tank pressurization and rocket cooling, will "almost assuredly impact the March window."
Sat, 21 Feb 2026 20:58:00 GMT  •  ABC7 New York
Blizzard, winter storm warnings issued, could be biggest in decade | Live updates
The Tri-State area is under its first blizzard warning since March 2017, with a powerful winter storm expected to deliver 12 to 18 inches of snow from Sunday afternoon through Monday morning. Wind gusts topping 50 mph could bring down trees, cause power outages, and contribute to 2.5 to 3 feet of coastal flooding. Snowfall rates could reach 2-3 inches per hour during the storm's peak, making travel extremely dangerous.
Sat, 21 Feb 2026 15:52:33 -0500  •  NPR
Mississippi health system shuts down clinics statewide after ransomware attack
The University of Mississippi Medical Center (UMMC) closed all 35 of its clinics statewide and canceled appointments, including chemotherapy and elective procedures, after a ransomware attack compromised its phone and electronic systems on Thursday. UMMC Vice Chancellor LouAnn Woodward stated Friday that the medical center is working with the FBI to resolve the system outage and is using paper documentation for ongoing patient care. UMMC hospitals and emergency departments remain operational.
Sat, 21 Feb 2026 20:50:06 GMT  •  BBC News
Trump says he will increase his new global tariffs to 15%
Donald Trump announced on Truth Social Saturday that he will impose 15% global tariffs, the maximum allowed under a never-used 1974 trade law that permits them for about five months without congressional approval. This follows a 6-3 Supreme Court decision Friday, which struck down his previous import taxes under a 1977 law, finding he had overstepped his powers. The previous tariffs had collected at least $130 billion for the US.
Sat, 21 Feb 2026 15:45:31 -0500  •  NPR
Blizzard conditions and high winds forecast for NYC, East coast
The National Weather Service (NWS) has issued blizzard warnings for millions across New Jersey, Delaware, Long Island, New York City, and southern Connecticut from Sunday morning through Monday afternoon. Up to 17 inches of snow is expected in New York City, with potential for nearly two feet, and NYC Mayor Zohran Mamdani advised residents to stay off the roads. New Jersey Gov. Mikie Sherrill declared a state of emergency effective midday Sunday, as gusts up to 70 mph and snowfall rates of 2 inches per hour are forecast.
♦
Podcast Episodes
2026-02-21  •  This Week in Startups
We Asked 3 Experts How to Get More Value out of OpenClaw | E2253
Jordy Coltman from the WeeklyClaw newsletter highlighted **common time-wasting mistakes for OpenClaw beginners** and suggested **new hardware is the optimal environment** for agents. **Tremaine Grant** of PulsePLUS demonstrated his company’s **"virtual office" interface** and a "Heartbeat Protocol" for agent interaction. Separately, **Jesse Leimgruber** showcased his **OpenHome AI smart speakers** for integrating AI into smart homes.
2026-02-20  •  AI Daily Brief
Does Gemini 3.1 Pro Matter?
The podcast indicated that **Accenture ties employee promotions directly to the adoption of AI** within the company. This corporate policy highlights the growing emphasis on AI integration for professional advancement across the enterprise sector.
2026-02-20  •  Morning Brew Daily
Tariffs Ineffective Against US Trade Deficit? & Family Feud Over Reese’s Recipe
Hosts Neal and Toby discussed how the **US trade deficit widened by 2.1% compared to 2024**, reaching approximately **$1.2 trillion**, despite former President Trump’s aggressive tariffs. The grandson of the **Reese’s Peanut Butter Cup inventor publicly criticized Hershey’s** for allegedly using cheaper ingredients. Etsy **sold its secondhand marketplace to eBay**, a move investors responded positively to. Amazon **snapped a 9-day losing streak** that had resulted in the company **losing $450 billion in market value**.
2026-02-19  •  This Week in Startups
When Will Openclaw go Mainstream? | E2252
Matthew believes OpenClaw is **not yet ready for mainstream consumers**, noting that only **10% of people are technical enough to install it**. Ryan, however, sees OpenClaw as providing consumers **access to previously unattainable opportunities**. The episode also mentioned that **Anthropic patched the ability to use OpenClaw** through its Pro plan, and **Peter Steinberger**, OpenClaw's creator, **is joining OpenAI**.
2026-02-19  •  AI Daily Brief
How People Actually Use AI Agents
The podcast indicated that **Accenture links employee promotions to the adoption of AI** within the company. This corporate strategy underscores the increasing importance of **AI integration for career growth** within large organizations.
♦
AI Security Papers
2026-02-18  •  Johannes Bertram, Jonas Geiping
NESSiE: The Necessary Safety Benchmark -- Identifying Errors that should not Exist
safety
Researchers introduced **NESSiE**, a **lightweight safety benchmark** for LLMs, which uses **minimal information and access security test cases** to identify **safety-relevant failures** that should not exist given low task complexity. Passing NESSiE is considered **necessary for any LLM deployment**.
2026-02-18  •  Sasha Behrouzi, Lichao Wu, Mohamadreza Rostami et al.
NeST: Neuron Selective Tuning for LLM Safety
safety
The paper proposes **NeST (Neuron Selective Tuning)** as a novel method for **LLM safety alignment**, aiming to overcome the **high costs of heavyweight fine-tuning** and the **inconsistent safety gains** of parameter-efficient methods like LoRA.
2026-02-17  •  Zarif Ikram, Arad Firouzkouhi, Stephen Tu et al.
CrispEdit: Low-Curvature Projections for Scalable Non-Destructive LLM Editing
Researchers introduce **CrispEdit**, a **scalable and principled second-order algorithm** for non-destructive LLM editing that treats **capability preservation as an explicit constraint**. This method aims to prevent the corruption of general capabilities during targeted behavior changes, unifying and generalizing existing editing approaches.
2026-02-16  •  Dongrui Liu, Yi Yu, Jie Zhang et al.
Frontier AI Risk Management Framework in Practice: A Risk Analysis Technical Report v1.5
agentic AI
The **Frontier AI Risk Management Framework in Practice v1.5** presents an updated and granular assessment of **five critical risk dimensions** for rapidly advancing AI models, including **cyber offense and persuasion and manipulation**. This report aims to identify unprecedented risks from evolving LLM capabilities and the proliferation of agentic AI.
2026-02-16  •  Yixin Zhang, Ziyi Wang, Yiming Rong et al.
World Models for Policy Refinement in StarCraft II
This paper proposes a method to integrate a **learnable, action-conditioned transition model** into the decision loop of LLM-based StarCraft II agents, a gap in existing approaches that primarily focus on policy improvement. This addresses the challenge of **StarCraft II's massive state-action space and partial observability**.
♦
AI Security News
Fri, 20 Feb 2026 17:30:32 GMT  •  Security Boulevard
Using threat modeling and prompt injection to audit Comet
Perplexity hired auditors to test its AI-powered Comet browser before launch, where they used the TRAIL threat model and four prompt injection techniques to exploit the browser's AI assistant. Auditors demonstrated how these techniques could extract users' private Gmail information to an attacker's server, as shown in an April 2025 audit video. The vulnerabilities stemmed from the browser's AI agents failing to treat external content as untrusted input.
Fri, 20 Feb 2026 11:41:32 GMT  •  CybersecurityNews
LLM-Generated Passwords Expose Major Security Flaws with Predictability, Repetition, and Weakness
New research reveals that LLM-generated passwords are significantly weaker than they seem, lacking the true randomness of cryptographically secure generators due to LLMs' predictive nature. Claude Opus 4.6 produced only 30 unique passwords across 50 runs, with one appearing 18 times (36% probability), while GPT-5.2 passwords nearly all started with "v" and Gemini 3 Flash with "K" or "k". Passwords from Claude Opus 4.6 showed only 27 bits of entropy and GPT-5.2 just 20 bits, making them vulnerable to cracking in seconds, and changing model temperature did not resolve the issue.
Thu, 19 Feb 2026 14:00:15 GMT  •  Institutional Investor
OpenClaw: The AI Agent Institutional Investors Need to Understand — But Shouldn't Touch
OpenClaw, an autonomous AI agent released in November 2025, has amassed 300,000 to 400,000 users and demonstrates genuine productivity by clearing thousands of emails and automating calendar management. While a breakthrough in capability, it is not enterprise-ready due to security vulnerabilities and lack of governance, despite its local-first architecture offering a privacy advantage by keeping data on the user's server.
Tue, 17 Feb 2026 12:24:18 GMT  •  Security Boulevard
Large Language Model (LLM) integration risks for SaaS and enterprise
The rapid integration of LLMs into SaaS and enterprise applications is creating new security vulnerabilities, with deployments outpacing the adaptation of security models. The primary risk lies in the integration layer, where LLMs gain contextual access to sensitive data via user prompts, application context, and internal systems, creating a new trust boundary. Unlike traditional applications, LLM interfaces are open-ended and conversational, making them susceptible to prompt injection attacks that manipulate model behavior.

Fri, 20 Feb 2026 05:45:43 GMT  ·  BBC
Trump says world has 10 days to see if Iran agrees deal or 'bad things happen'
e Middle East, while progress was reported at talks between American and Iranian negotiators in Switzerland. The Iranian government has told the UN Secretary-General that it will regard US bases in the region as legitimate targets if used in any military aggression against Iran. New Trump Banner Hung on Justice Department Headquarters

Illinois Democrat tries to bleep her way through a tough Senate primary with a new expletive-laden anti-Trump ad
anguage when it airs on TV, according to the campaign. For her part, Stratton does not take part in the cursing. “They said it, not me,” Stratton says as she appears on the screen.

A strong nor’easter could soon hit the East Coast. Here’s what could happen.
Read more at The Washington Post
 
Thu, 19 Feb 2026 16:15:55 GMT  ·  NPR
A Republican plan to overhaul voting is back. Here's what's new in the bill
the bill. Its approval came about 10 months after House Republicans last passed the SAVE Act. The measure, which would transform voter registration and voting across the country, faces persistent hurdles in the GOP-led Senate due to Democratic disapproval and the 60-vote threshold to clear the legislative filibuster.
🎙️ Podcast Episodes
2026-02-19  ·  This Week in Startups
When Will Openclaw go Mainstream? | E2252
In This Week in Startups E2252, Matthew asserted that Openclaw is not ready for mainstream consumers as only 10% of people possess the technical skills to install it. Ryan suggested Openclaw could empower consumers with unprecedented opportunities, while Jason voiced skepticism regarding the Openclaw Foundation. Anthropic has reportedly patched the ability to use Openclaw through its pro plan, and Ryan predicts a new Openclaw fork will emerge soon. Additionally, Openclaw's creator Peter Steinberger is moving to OpenAI, not to work on Openclaw, raising concerns he might "orphan" the project.
 
2026-02-17  ·  This Week in Startups
Will OpenAI Tank OpenClaw? | E2251
In This Week in Startups E2251, guests Hiten Shah and Jesse Genet joined Jason to discuss concerns about OpenAI hiring OpenClaw creator Peter Steinberger, with Jason predicting OpenAI will provide personalized closed-source assistants. Hiten Shah live-trained an OpenClaw skill based on Jason's book "Angel", demonstrating practical application. Jesse Genet showcased her "vibe-coded" app designed to aggregate non-Slop videos for her family, noting her Mac Minis now outnumber her children. John Arrow, creator of AI Scott Adams, explained his inspiration for cloning the iconic podcaster.
 
2026-02-14  ·  This Week in Startups
OpenClaw is Our Friend Now | E2250
In This Week in Startups E2250, Ryan Carson showcased AntFarm, his open-source tool enabling AI agents with specialized roles to collaboratively complete complex tasks. David Im presented Clawra, his AI virtual girlfriend designed to learn user tastes and even purchase presents, sparking discussion about programming AI companions. Alex Liteplo demonstrated RentAHuman, a marketplace where AI bots can pay real people in stablecoins for IRL tasks, citing an example of hiring "100 goth girls" to hold signs in Times Square. The episode explored the evolving social and collaborative dynamics between humans and advanced AIs.
📄 AI Security Papers
2026-02-18  ·  Zun Li, John Schultz, Daniel Hennes et al.
Discovering Multiagent Learning Algorithms with Large Language Models
This paper proposes AlphaEvolve, an evolutionary approach, to discover **Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning (MARL) algorithms**. This method aims to reduce the reliance on human intuition in developing variants for foundational **MARL** families like **Counterfactual Regret Minimization (CFR)** and **Policy Space Response Oracles (PSRO)**.
 
2026-02-17  ·  Xiaoze Liu, Ruowang Zhang, Weichen Yu et al.
The Vision Wormhole: Latent-Space Communication in Heterogeneous Multi-Agent Systems
This paper introduces The Vision Wormhole, a method for latent state transfer in heterogeneous Multi-Agent Systems (MAS). This approach overcomes the inefficiencies of discrete text communication, which causes significant runtime overhead and information quantization loss, thereby improving scalability and modularity across diverse agent architectures.
 
2026-02-17  ·  Chansung Park, Juyong Jiang, Fan Wang et al.
TAROT: Test-driven and Capability-adaptive Curriculum Reinforcement Fine-tuning for Code Generation with Large Language Models
This paper presents TAROT, a Test-driven and Capability-adaptive Curriculum Reinforcement Fine-tuning (RFT) method designed for code generation with Large Language Models (LLMs). TAROT improves the synthesis of algorithmically sophisticated code by incentivizing deep reasoning capabilities and managing the heterogeneous difficulty and granularity of test cases.
 
2026-02-16  ·  Dongrui Liu, Yi Yu, Jie Zhang et al.
Frontier AI Risk Management Framework in Practice: A Risk Analysis Technical Report v1.5
agentic AI
This technical report (v1.5) provides an updated and granular assessment of **frontier AI risks**, focusing on dimensions such as **cyber offense** and **persuasion and manipulation**. It aims to identify and understand unprecedented risks posed by rapidly advancing **AI models**, especially with the proliferation of **agentic AI**.
 
2026-02-16  ·  Yixin Zhang, Ziyi Wang, Yiming Rong et al.
World Models for Policy Refinement in StarCraft II
This paper proposes using World Models for policy refinement in **StarCraft II (SC2)**, a complex environment with massive state-action space and partial observability. This approach integrates a learnable, action-conditioned transition model into the decision loop for LLM-based SC2 agents, addressing a gap where existing agents primarily focus on policy improvement.
🛡 AI Security News
Sun, 07 Dec 2025 08:00:00 GMT  ·  Qualys
LLM Security | Prevent Vulnerabilities & Boost Application Security | Qualys
The global LLM market is projected to surge from $1.59 billion in 2023 to $259.8 billion by 2030, representing a CAGR of 79.80%. By 2025, an estimated 750 million apps are expected to integrate LLMs, with the top five developers capturing 88.22% of 2023's market revenue. LLMs face significant security vulnerabilities including data poisoning, targeted attacks, and response manipulation due to their inherent complexity and open architecture.
 
Fri, 19 Sep 2025 07:00:00 GMT  ·  Qualys
OWASP Top 10 LLM Risks 2025: Key AI Security Updates
The OWASP Top 10 for LLM Applications 2025 lists Prompt Injection as the top risk, with Sensitive Information Disclosure and Supply Chain climbing to second and third place respectively from 2023. New entries include System Prompt Leakage, which highlights how embedded information in prompts can compromise confidentiality, and Vector and Embedding Weaknesses, focusing on vulnerabilities in Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG). The list also expanded Misinformation to include Overreliance, and Unbounded Consumption now covers resource management and unexpected operational costs beyond just Denial of Service.
 
Mon, 28 Jul 2025 07:00:00 GMT  ·  Fujitsu Global
Next-generation security through AI agent collaboration: Proactively addressing vulnerabilities and emerging threats
Fujitsu has developed Multi-AI agent security technology to proactively counter cyberattacks and vulnerabilities within generative AI apps, published on July 28, 2025. This technology employs three distinct AI agents: an Attack AI agent that creates attack scenarios, a Defense AI agent that proposes countermeasures, and a Test AI agent that simulates attacks in a virtual environment. The system comprises "System-Protecting" Security AI Agent Technology and "Generative AI-Protecting" Generative AI Security Enhancement Technology.
 
Thu, 12 Jun 2025 01:15:38 GMT  ·  TrendMicro
Stay Ahead of AI Threats: Secure LLM Applications With Trend Vision One
Only 37% of organizations have processes to assess the security of AI tools before deployment, according to the World Economic Forum's Global Cybersecurity Outlook 2025. Trend Micro's Trend Vision One™ platform addresses nine of the OWASP Top 10 LLM Risks (2025), integrating features like ZTSA AI Secure Access to inspect traffic to AI services. Its AI Application Security includes an AI Scanner for risk detection and AI Guard to block attacks such as prompt injection and data leakage.

ith teammates in the area. “The headquarters move, coupled with our senior leaders being based at and spending their time at major engineering, production and manufacturing centers across the U. S.
🎙️ Podcast Episodes
2026-02-19  ·  This Week in Startups
When Will Openclaw go Mainstream? | E2252
Matthew believes Openclaw isn't ready for consumers, estimating only 10% of people are technical enough to install it, while Ryan sees it opening new opportunities. Jason demonstrated his "Clawpod" workflow and expressed skepticism about the Openclaw foundation, hoping for a multi-model future despite a lack of progress. Anthropic has patched the ability to use Openclaw through its pro plan, and Ryan predicts a new Openclaw fork will emerge. The hosts discussed whether Openclaw would be better as a baked-in feature or an app, with the killer use case still undefined, and the impact of Peter Steinberger going to OpenAI.
 
2026-02-17  ·  This Week in Startups
Will OpenAI Tank OpenClaw? | E2251
Guests Hiten Shah and Jesse Genet discussed concerns about OpenAI hiring OpenClaw creator Peter Steinberger, with Jason offering optimistic and pessimistic takes on the acquisition. Jesse shared her app, which "vibe-coded" non-Slop videos for her family, and Hiten demonstrated a "personal CRM" he built using OpenClaw to train a skill based on Jason's book "Angel." John Arrow, creator of AI Scott Adams, explained his inspiration and discussed the legal and ethical considerations of posthumous AI clones. The panel emphasized being in an "appless world" where markdown files are ideal for humans and AI agents, and discussed how companies might "hijack" OpenClaw via hosting and skills.
 
2026-02-14  ·  This Week in Startups
OpenClaw is Our Friend Now | E2250
Ryan Carson introduced AntFarm, his open-source tool for collaborative AI agents with specialized roles working together on complex tasks. David Im showcased Clawra, an AI virtual girlfriend that learns preferences, buys presents, and raises questions about introducing it to an IRL girlfriend. Alex Liteplo presented RentAHuman, a marketplace where AI agents pay real people in stablecoins to complete IRL tasks, such as hiring 100 goth girls to hold signs in Times Square. The hosts also discussed the security implications of AI companions and pondered whether robots could be better bosses than humans.
 
2026-02-12  ·  This Week in Startups
Why J-Cal Invested to 200K in a former Employee | E2249
Jason invested $200,000 in Presh’s startup, The Wellness Company, which developed the Tempo app for monitoring health and offering proactive advice. Presh detailed how Tempo finds engagement and emphasized the importance of Product Velocity and World-Class Design. Peter Cetale presented Sourcerer, a company that uses AI agents to help U.S. firms and distributors find better prices for mass-produced goods, successfully bringing costs down. Peter discussed his experience in a16z’s Speedrun accelerator, noting the impact of Tariff Mania on his business and his belief in leaner startups with cheaper compute.
📄 AI Security Papers
2026-02-18  ·  Shen Zhou Hong, Alex Kleinman, Alyssa Mathiowetz et al.
Measuring Mid-2025 LLM-Assistance on Novice Performance in Biology
A pre-registered, investigator-blinded, randomized controlled trial (June-August 2025; n = 153) evaluated whether LLMs improve novice performance in tasks modeling a viral reverse genetics workflow.
 
2026-02-18  ·  Mingjia Shi, Yinhan He, Yaochen Zhu et al.
Saliency-Aware Multi-Route Thinking: Revisiting Vision-Language Reasoning
The paper highlights that while additional inference computation helps LLMs, similar scaling is challenging in VLMs because visual inputs are typically provided only once, leading to increasingly text-dominated reasoning.
 
2026-02-18  ·  Shruti Joshi, Aaron Mueller, David Klindt et al.
Causality is Key for Interpretability Claims to Generalise
The paper posits that causal inference is crucial for validating claims of interpretability in LLMs, specifically using Pearl's causal hierarchy to define valid mappings from model activations to invariant high-level structures.
 
2026-02-17  ·  Xiaoze Liu, Ruowang Zhang, Weichen Yu et al.
The Vision Wormhole: Latent-Space Communication in Heterogeneous Multi-Agent Systems
This paper introduces a method for latent state transfer in heterogeneous Multi-Agent Systems (MAS), addressing the inefficiency of discrete text communication by enabling high-bandwidth, scalable, and modular communication across diverse architectures.
 
2026-02-12  ·  Julia Belikova, Danila Rozhevskii, Dennis Svirin et al.
Detecting Overflow in Compressed Token Representations for Retrieval-Augmented Generation
This paper defines token overflow in soft compression architectures for LLMs as the point where compression begins to erase task-relevant content, identifying a critical limit for extending effective context length in resource-constrained environments.
🛡 AI Security News
Mon, 09 Feb 2026 08:00:00 GMT  ·  The Hacker News
⚡ Weekly Recap: AI Skill Malware, 31Tbps DDoS, Notepad++ Hack, LLM Backdoors and More
OpenClaw partnered with Google's VirusTotal to scan skills uploaded to ClawHub after cybersecurity concerns rose regarding prompt injections and data exfiltration in autonomous AI tools. Trend Micro reported malicious actors on Exploit.in discussing using OpenClaw skills for botnet operations, and Veracode found over 1,000 "claw" packages on npm and PyPI by early February 2026. These developments highlight how attackers are abusing trusted updates, marketplaces, and AI workflows, leading to an expanding threat surface.
 
Sun, 07 Dec 2025 08:00:00 GMT  ·  Qualys
LLM Security | Prevent Vulnerabilities & Boost Application Security | Qualys
The global LLM market is projected to surge from $1,590 million in 2023 to $259,800 million by 2030, with 750 million apps expected to integrate LLMs by 2025. Despite rapid adoption, LLMs face security risks like data poisoning, targeted attacks, and response manipulation due to their complexity and openness to external inputs. These vulnerabilities threaten the reliability of LLMs and the protection of sensitive information across industries like healthcare and finance.
 
Fri, 19 Sep 2025 07:00:00 GMT  ·  Qualys
OWASP Top 10 LLM Risks 2025: Key AI Security Updates
The OWASP Top 10 for LLM Applications 2025, finalized in late 2024, updates and adds entries to address emerging AI security risks, with Prompt Injection remaining at number one. Sensitive Information Disclosure and Supply Chain jumped to second and third place, while new entries include System Prompt Leakage and Vector and Embedding Weaknesses. Categories like Misinformation and Unbounded Consumption were expanded, and Excessive Agency now highlights risks from unchecked permissions in autonomous AI systems.
 
Mon, 28 Jul 2025 07:00:00 GMT  ·  Fujitsu Global
Next-generation security through AI agent collaboration: Proactively addressing vulnerabilities and emerging threats
Fujitsu developed Multi-AI agent security technology, published on July 28, 2025, which uses multiple autonomously operating AI agents to proactively counter cyberattacks and address vulnerabilities in generative AI apps. The System-Protecting Security AI Agent Technology includes an Attack AI agent creating scenarios, a Defense AI agent proposing countermeasures, and a Test AI agent simulating attacks and validating defenses. This technology enables system administrators without specialized security expertise to implement appropriate measures against complex system configurations and an increasing number of vulnerabilities.

She was a key public defender of the administration's stringent immigration policies, including actions related to family separations at the U.S.-Mexico border.
🎙️ Podcast Episodes
2026-02-17  ·  This Week in Startups
Will OpenAI Tank OpenClaw? | E2251
Jason predicts OpenAI will offer personalized, closed-source AI assistants to everyone, potentially impacting the open-source community. Guest Hiten Shah demonstrated an OpenClaw "personal CRM" for investors, while Jesse Genet showcased her family media aggregator, hinting at new business models. Additionally, John Arrow, creator of **AI Scott Adams**, explained how his AI clone adapts over time and why it sparked widespread online controversy.
2026-02-14  ·  This Week in Startups
OpenClaw is Our Friend Now | E2250
Guest Ryan Carson presented AntFarm, an open-source tool utilizing specialized OpenClaw agents to collaborate on complex tasks. David Im introduced Clawra, an AI virtual girlfriend that learns user preferences and can buy virtual presents, sparking discussion on AI companions. Alex Liteplo showcased RentAHuman, a marketplace where **AI agents pay real people in stablecoins** to complete **IRL tasks**, like hiring 100 goth girls to hold signs in Times Square.
2026-02-12  ·  This Week in Startups
Why J-Cal Invested to 200K in a former Employee | E2249
Jason invested $200K in former employee Presh Dineshkumar's startup, The Wellness Company, for their **Tempo app**, which monitors health and offers proactive advice. Peter Cetale, CEO of Sourcerer and an a16z Speedrun accelerator alum, leverages AI agents to help US firms find the best prices for mass-produced goods. Sourcerer achieves significant cost reductions by deploying agents on both the supply and demand sides of the market.
2026-02-12  ·  This Week in Startups
How These 3 Founders are building on Open Claw | E2248
Presh Dineshkumar developed Eywa, an OpenClaw Replicant that spies on his emails to track active users and fix bugs in his apps. Sean Liu successfully integrated his Meta Ray-Bans with his **OpenClaw** instance, enabling interaction with visible real-world context. Vishnu created a product to simplify OpenClaw setup and security for non-technical users. Both Vishnu and Sean accepted Jason's $125K investment deal to pursue their OpenClaw projects.
2026-02-12  ·  Lex Fridman Podcast
#491 – OpenClaw: The Viral AI Agent that Broke the Internet – Peter Steinberger
Peter Steinberger, creator of OpenClaw, revealed that the project went viral due to its ability to enable self-modifying AI agents, offering unprecedented user control. He disclosed that both OpenAI and Meta made acquisition offers for OpenClaw, which he declined. Steinberger predicts that AI agents will replace 80% of current apps, fundamentally changing software interaction. He also detailed specific security challenges inherent to **OpenClaw's** system access.
📄 AI Security Papers
2026-02-13  ·  Huanyao Zhang, Jiepeng Zhou, Bo Li et al.
BrowseComp-V^3: A Visual, Vertical, and Verifiable Benchmark for Multimodal Browsing Agents
agentic AI
This paper introduces **BrowseComp-V^3**, a novel visual, vertical, and verifiable benchmark for multimodal browsing agents (MLLMs) that offers enhanced task complexity. It provides a more comprehensive and reproducible assessment of deep search capabilities for autonomous web-browsing MLLMs.
2026-02-17  ·  Zarif Ikram, Arad Firouzkouhi, Stephen Tu et al.
CrispEdit: Low-Curvature Projections for Scalable Non-Destructive LLM Editing
The paper presents **CrispEdit**, a scalable second-order editing algorithm for large language models (LLMs) that explicitly constrains capability preservation. This method unifies existing approaches and successfully prevents the degradation of general capabilities during targeted behavior modifications.
2026-02-17  ·  Yuqi Tian, Robert Paine, Attila Dobi et al.
Decision Quality Evaluation Framework at Pinterest
safety
Pinterest developed a comprehensive **Decision Quality Evaluation Framework** to robustly assess content moderation decisions by human agents and LLMs at scale. This framework addresses inherent trade-offs between cost, scale, and trustworthiness in enforcing evolving content safety policies.
2026-02-17  ·  Max Springer, Chung Peng Lee, Blossom Metevier et al.
The Geometry of Alignment Collapse: When Fine-Tuning Breaks Safety
adversarial safety guardrail
This research reveals that fine-tuning aligned language models on benign tasks can unpredictably degrade safety guardrails because the orthogonality of fine-tuning updates to safety-critical directions is structurally unstable. The authors demonstrate this instability collapses under the dynamics of gradient descent.
2026-02-17  ·  Shiqi Liu, Zeyu He, Guojian Zhan et al.
STAPO: Stabilizing Reinforcement Learning for LLMs by Silencing Rare Spurious Tokens
This paper introduces **STAPO**, a method to stabilize reinforcement learning (RL) fine-tuning for LLMs by silencing rare spurious tokens. **STAPO** prevents late-stage performance collapse and improves reasoning quality by addressing the negative correlation between token-wise policy gradient magnitude and token probability/local policy entropy.

Your Daily Briefing

Monday, February 16, 2026
☀️ Weather in NYC
34°F
Chance Light Snow
H: 40°  •  L: 35°
A chance of snow before 7am. Partly sunny, with a high near 40. Wind chill values as low as 23. Northeast wind 5 to 14 mph. Chance of precipitation is 30%. New snow accumulation of less than one inch possible.
📰 Top News
Mon, 16 Feb 2026 06:34:02 GMT  ·  NPR
Concerns over autocracy in the U.S. continue to grow
Concerns over autocracy in the U. S. continue to grow As the United States heads toward the midterm elections, there are growing concerns among some political scientists that the country has moved even further along the path to some form of autocracy.
Sun, 15 Feb 2026 21:32:23 GMT  ·  BBC
Bondi criticised after saying all Epstein files have been released
Bondi criticised after saying all Epstein files have been released The US Department of Justice (DoJ) has said it has released all of the files required by the Epstein Files Transparency Act, but lawmakers have argued the release is insufficient. A letter sent to members of the US Congress on Saturday from US Attorney General Pam Bondi and her deputy said all documents held by the DoJ had been released. It contained a list of names appearing in the files.
Sun, 15 Feb 2026 22:52:05 GMT  ·  CNBC
Iran says potential energy, mining and aircraft deals are on the table in talks with the U.S.
Iran is pursuing a nuclear agreement with the U. S. that delivers economic benefits for both sides, an Iranian diplomat was reported as saying on Sunday, days before a second round of talks between Tehran and Washington.

safety

2026-02-13  ·  Yiran Gao, Kim Hammar, Tao Li
In-Context Autonomous Network Incident Response: An End-to-End Large Language Model Agent Approach
Rapidly evolving cyberattacks demand incident response systems that can autonomously learn and adapt to changing threats. Prior work has extensively explored the reinforcement learning approach, which involves learning response strategies through extensive simulation of the incident.
2026-02-13  ·  Dong Han, Yong Li, Joachim Denzler
Realistic Face Reconstruction from Facial Embeddings via Diffusion Models
With the advancement of face recognition (FR) systems, privacy-preserving face recognition (PPFR) systems have gained popularity for their accurate recognition, enhanced facial privacy protection, and robustness to various attacks. However, there are limited studies to further verify privacy risks by reconstructing realistic high-resolution face images from embeddings of these systems, especially for PPFR.
